title: Hirschfeld's study Die Transvestiten coins the term transvestitism
description: Magnus Hirschfeld published Die Transvestiten, a study classifying cross-dressing that introduced the term transvestitism. It was among the earliest medical works to treat gender variance as a distinct subject.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Hirschfeld founds the Institute for Sexual Science in Berlin
description: Magnus Hirschfeld opened the Institute for Sexual Science in Berlin, which grew into an internationally significant centre for the study of sexuality and hosted the world's first gender identity clinic.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Nazis destroy Hirschfeld's Institute for Sexual Science
description: The Nazi regime destroyed Magnus Hirschfeld's Institute for Sexual Science in Berlin, ending its research and dispersing its library and archives.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: First documented gender reassignment surgery on Dora Richter
description: Doctors Ludwig Levy-Lenz and Erwin Gohrbandt performed the first documented gender reassignment surgery on Dora Richter, who worked at Hirschfeld's Institute for Sexual Science. She became the first transgender woman documented to undergo a vaginoplasty.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Gillies and Millard perform a phalloplasty on Michael Dillon
description: Sir Harold Gillies and Ralph Millard carried out a pedicle-flap phalloplasty on Dr Michael Dillon in the United Kingdom. The reconstructive technique, developed for injured soldiers, was offered as a genital surgery option for transgender men.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Christine Jorgensen's transition draws worldwide attention
description: Christine Jorgensen, a US Army veteran who underwent gender confirmation surgery in Denmark, became the first transgender woman whose transition was widely publicised by the media. She spoke openly about hormone therapy and surgery and became an advocate for transgender people.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Harry Benjamin coins the term transsexual
description: Endocrinologist and sexologist Harry Benjamin introduced the term transsexual, distinguishing the concept from transvestitism. His clinical approach later took precedence over psychoanalytic treatment.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Harry Benjamin publishes The Transsexual Phenomenon
description: Harry Benjamin published The Transsexual Phenomenon, which separated transvestitism from transsexuality and argued for acceptance of gender-affirming surgery. The book shaped clinical treatment of transgender people for decades.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Alfred Kinsey publishes his human sexual behaviour studies
description: Alfred Kinsey published his research on the sexual behaviour of human males and females, criticising the tendency of scientists to portray homosexuals and heterosexuals as inherently different types of people. The work later influenced the reclassification of homosexuality.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Evelyn Hooker's study challenges pathologisation of homosexuality
description: Psychologist Evelyn Hooker published an influential paper showing that homosexual men are as psychologically well-adjusted as heterosexual men. The finding became a major factor in later reclassification of homosexuality by psychiatry.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: American Psychiatric Association removes homosexuality from the DSM
description: The American Psychiatric Association voted to remove homosexuality from its list of recognised psychiatric disorders in the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders. The decision drew on research by Evelyn Hooker and Alfred Kinsey.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: WHO removes homosexuality from the International Classification of Diseases
description: The World Health Organization removed homosexuality from the list of mental disorders in its International Classification of Diseases, ending its global medical classification as an illness.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: First cases of AIDS are recorded
description: Five previously healthy gay men in Los Angeles presented with the rare lung infection Pneumocystis carinii pneumonia, while men in New York and California were reported with the aggressive skin cancer Kaposi's Sarcoma. These were among the first recorded cases of AIDS.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Terrence Higgins Trust founded after the death of Terrence Higgins
description: Terrence Higgins, one of the first British men known to die of an AIDS-related illness, died in 1982. His partner and friends established the Terrence Higgins Trust, which became a leading sexual health and HIV charity in the United Kingdom.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Pasteur Institute researchers isolate HIV
description: Researchers at the Pasteur Institute in Paris isolated the Human Immunodeficiency Virus, which the international scientific community recognised as the cause of AIDS in 1986.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: First commercial AIDS antibody blood test is licensed
description: The US Food and Drug Administration licensed ELISA, the first commercial AIDS antibody blood test. HIV testing and counselling services were introduced and blood donations began to be routinely screened.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: UK funds SIGMA study into gay men and AIDS
description: The UK Department of Health funded SIGMA, the Socio-sexual Investigations of Gay Men and AIDS, to offer HIV testing in non-clinical settings. The project ran until 1995 and evolved into Sigma Research, which studies LGBT health and wellbeing.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: World AIDS Summit held in London
description: The World Health Organization and the UK Government co-hosted the World AIDS Summit in London, the first time nations gathered to discuss AIDS, drawing health ministers from 148 countries. The London Declaration on AIDS Prevention protected individuals from discrimination in prevention programmes.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: First World AIDS Day is observed
description: The first World AIDS Day, initiated by the World Health Organization, was observed to raise awareness of AIDS. It has been marked internationally on this date each year since.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: UK Department of Health forms the PRISM staff LGBT network
description: The UK Department of Health established PRISM, one of the first staff LGBT networks in a UK public body.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Gay and Lesbian Association of Doctors and Dentists is founded
description: The Gay and Lesbian Association of Doctors and Dentists (GLADD) was established in the United Kingdom. Its work included the Dignity at Work document and a report on support for healthcare professionals living with HIV.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: LGBT History Month first celebrated in the UK
description: LGBT History Month was celebrated for the first time in the United Kingdom, marking an annual observance of LGBT history and contributions.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: BMA publishes a celebration of LGBT doctors in the NHS
description: The British Medical Association published a publication celebrating the contributions of LGBT doctors to the NHS to mark LGBT History Month.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Highly Active Antiretroviral Therapy enters early development
description: Highly Active Antiretroviral Therapy (HAART), combining antiretroviral drugs including protease inhibitors to suppress HIV replication, entered early development. It would transform the prognosis of HIV and AIDS, and within a year could be taken as a single daily tablet.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: First rapid HIV test is approved
description: The first rapid HIV test was approved, offering a result within 20 minutes at 99.6 percent accuracy and expanding access to testing.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: FDA approves Truvada for HIV prevention
description: The US Food and Drug Administration approved Truvada as the first drug to prevent HIV acquisition, taken daily by those at risk. It marked the start of pre-exposure prophylaxis as an HIV prevention tool.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: First annual LGBT Health Summit held in the UK
description: The first annual LGBT Health Summit, discussing the health issues of LGBT people in the United Kingdom, was held in London.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: First UK Department of Health working group on trans issues
description: The first UK Department of Health working group on trans issues was formed, chaired by Christine Burns as part of the Sexual Orientation and Gender Identity Advisory Group, later succeeded by the LGBT Advisory Group.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Human Fertilisation and Embryology Act gives lesbians equal legal parentage
description: The Human Fertilisation and Embryology Act provided equal access to legal parentage for lesbians and their partners in relation to IVF and assisted or self insemination in the United Kingdom.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Same-sex couples and single people gain adoption rights in England
description: After the Adoption and Children Act removed the condition that only married couples could adopt, same-sex couples and single people regardless of sexual orientation became able to adopt or foster children in England.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Adoption reform passed in full in Scotland
description: The adoption reform allowing same-sex couples and single people to adopt was passed in full in Scotland.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Northern Ireland allows adoption by couples in civil partnerships
description: Northern Ireland amended its law so that couples in a civil partnership could adopt children. Previously only a single gay or lesbian person could adopt there.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Transgender EuroStudy on healthcare access is published
description: The Transgender EuroStudy: Legal Survey and Focus on Transgender Experience of Health Care was published, described as the largest study of trans people's experiences of accessing healthcare services in Europe.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Stonewall publishes Prescription for Change
description: Stonewall published Prescription for Change, a national report on the neglected healthcare needs and experiences of over 6,000 lesbian and bisexual women in the United Kingdom.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: US Institute of Medicine reports on LGBT health disparities
description: The US Institute of Medicine published Health of Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, and Transgender People, a landmark report assessing health disparities among LGBT people and setting out a research agenda for the National Institutes of Health.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Stonewall publishes a major survey of gay and bisexual men's health
description: Stonewall published a survey of gay and bisexual men's health needs with 6,861 respondents across Britain. It found that many needs went unmet, with particular concern over mental health and drug use.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: UK Government publishes the National LGBT Survey
description: The UK Government Equalities Office published the National LGBT Survey, launched in July 2017, gathering the experiences of over 108,000 LGBT people across safety, health, education and employment. It was the largest national survey of LGBT people to date.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Stonewall publishes LGBT in Britain: Health Report
description: Stonewall published LGBT in Britain: Health Report, based on a survey of more than 5,000 LGBT people in the United Kingdom, examining mental health, wellbeing and experiences of discrimination when seeking medical support.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: UK Government publishes the LGBT Action Plan
description: In response to the National LGBT Survey, the Government Equalities Office published the LGBT Action Plan, setting out commitments to improve the lives of LGBT people in the United Kingdom.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: UK appoints its first National Advisor for LGBT Health
description: The UK Government appointed Dr Michael Brady, medical director of the Terrence Higgins Trust, as the first National Advisor for LGBT Health in the NHS, alongside a new expert advisory panel, to tackle inequality and improve access in the healthcare system.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Government Equalities Office hosts its first national LGBT conference
description: The UK Government Equalities Office held its inaugural national LGBT conference.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: ILC UK research reveals health disparities among older LGBT people
description: The International Longevity Centre UK, with researchers at University College London and Cardiff University, published findings showing that a lifetime of prejudice and stigma leaves older LGBT people with worse physical and mental health, poorer access to care, and greater social isolation.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History
